steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Tannen argues that the debate-driven approach "can be less informative than the presentation of multiple perspectives in a noncompetitive format." In other words, a multi-perspective, noncompetitive format (like a panel) should leave audiences better informed than a head-to-head debate.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
• A: measures perceived knowledge of commentators, not how informed the audience became — Tannen's claim is about audience understanding.
  • B: also about perceived knowledge, and it doesn't compare to the debate format at all.
  • C: shows the panel (noncompetitive, multiperspective) left viewers actually more informed than the debate — exactly Tannen's prediction.
  • D: compares the single commentator to the debate, ignoring the panel that Tannen's hypothesis hinges on.
That confirms C is correct.
